$meta.step: Step 2 — Design System and UX Foundation
$meta.description: Light-theme elevation levels. Elevation is spatial and never semantic.

elevation.0.description: Elevation level 0. Flat. The default for content on the page surface. Shadow values are tuned for the light theme only; a dark theme would require its own values and is PLANNED, NOT IMPLEMENTED.
prohibitedUsage: Never used to convey status, selection, validity or error — elevation is spatial, not semantic.

elevation.1.description: Elevation level 1. Cards and list containers at rest. Shadow values are tuned for the light theme only; a dark theme would require its own values and is PLANNED, NOT IMPLEMENTED.
prohibitedUsage: Never used to convey status, selection, validity or error — elevation is spatial, not semantic.

elevation.2.description: Elevation level 2. Raised cards, sticky headers. Shadow values are tuned for the light theme only; a dark theme would require its own values and is PLANNED, NOT IMPLEMENTED.
prohibitedUsage: Never used to convey status, selection, validity or error — elevation is spatial, not semantic.

elevation.3.description: Elevation level 3. Bottom sheets, dropdown menus, popovers. Shadow values are tuned for the light theme only; a dark theme would require its own values and is PLANNED, NOT IMPLEMENTED.
prohibitedUsage: Never used to convey status, selection, validity or error — elevation is spatial, not semantic.

elevation.4.description: Elevation level 4. Dialogs and modal surfaces. Shadow values are tuned for the light theme only; a dark theme would require its own values and is PLANNED, NOT IMPLEMENTED.
prohibitedUsage: Never used to convey status, selection, validity or error — elevation is spatial, not semantic.

elevation.5.description: Elevation level 5. Reserved. Not used by any Step 2 component. Shadow values are tuned for the light theme only; a dark theme would require its own values and is PLANNED, NOT IMPLEMENTED.
prohibitedUsage: Never used to convey status, selection, validity or error — elevation is spatial, not semantic.
